solution: 
```cpp
class SeatManager {
private:
    priority_queue<int, vector<int>, greater<int>> availableSeats;

public:
    SeatManager(int n) {
        for (int i = 1; i <= n; ++i) {
            availableSeats.push(i);
        }
    }
    
    int reserve() {
        int seat = availableSeats.top();
        availableSeats.pop();
        return seat;
    }
    
    void unreserve(int seatNumber) {
        availableSeats.push(seatNumber);
    }
};

/**
 * Your SeatManager object will be instantiated and called as such:
 * SeatManager* obj = new SeatManager(n);
 * int param_1 = obj->reserve();
 * obj->unreserve(seatNumber);
 */
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n) for reserve and unreserve
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a min-heap (priority_queue) to efficiently manage available seats. reserve() fetches the smallest-numbered seat in O(log n) time, and unreserve() adds a seat back to the heap in O(log n) time. The heap ensures optimal performance for both operations.
